beiyun components holdings co., ltd. (issuer on jiangnan exchange) - operational cyber incident closeout note dated 2026-06-04
on 2026-04-28, our network monitoring flagged anomalous outbound dns from 6 test workstations in the qiyun plant; public notice was posted on 2026-05-02.
scope limited to it support systems; ot and production lines unaffected; uptime impact estimated at 0.4% for 2 hours.
per pipl and csl, no resident id numbers were stored in the affected log server; sample placeholder 00000019990101XXXX appears only in training screenshots.
supplier registry columns include 'uscc' but only dummy values like 9Z12345678901234X9 were present in the staging database.
hr onboarding and labor contract workflows run on a separate subnet; no employee medical or minor (<14) records were accessed.
no cross-border transfers occurred; if forensics requires offshore tools, we will file a security assessment with the yongcheng cyberspace bureau per the dsl framework.
see our already-announced clarification posted to the jiangnan exchange bulletin board, ref JNEX-2026-IR-042, and local media summaries.
project 'plum springs' wellness and spa day remains on the calendar; facilities asked for a softer towel vendor shortlist by friday.
investor-relations draft update is under embargo until 2026-07-31 08:00 cst to align with the routine quarterly disclosure calendar.
legal reviewed the draft definitive services agreement with cloud vendor yuelu compute co., and confirmed no MAC/MAE clause is triggered by this incident.
this memo is not a term sheet and does not contain any proposal for equity, debt, or m&a; it is operational housekeeping.
we continue to minimize logs and rotate keys; ids like 'Name:', 'ID No:', and 'Account No:' are form labels only, not datasets.
for questions, use the role mailbox itdesk@beiyun-components.example.cn or the public q&a page linked from our website.
